results.question: bachelor party spot, loud, handles large groups
results.answer:
Jacques-Imo's Cafe is a fantastic spot for a bachelor party, especially if you're looking for a place that can handle large groups. The atmosphere is loud and rowdy, perfect for a fun night out. The food is exceptional, with huge portions and a variety of Southern specialties to try. Just be prepared for a wait, as it can get very crowded.

Acme Oyster House is another great choice, especially if you're looking for a place that can accommodate a large group. The food is delicious, and the service is friendly. It's a bit more straightforward, with no reservations needed, making it a bit more flexible for spontaneous plans.
contexts:
The first time I heard of this place, was years ago on TV.  Since, I had many many friends tell me that it was an experience that I had to enjoy for myself.  So... I went, and it was worth it.

A group of my friends went on a bachelor party weekend.  We ordered a disgusting amount of food, allowing us to sample everything.  I tried the fried chicken on numerous recomendations- as well as the red fish.  Both were fantastic.  

The atmosphere, the service, and the food all combined for a great experience.

Huge hit with our friends and still our favorite place in New Orleans. Warning--IT'S CROWDED! We faced a two hour wait had we not had reservations for six (so grab some more friends and a reservation). 

Bar is jammed and the tenders always pour heavy. Jack runs an old-school place to please his patrons.

The walk through the kitchen is a great touch. It's well run and clean. Atmosphere feeds off the kitchen. It's loud and even a bit rowdy. Bachelor party next to us was feeling no pain and the best man got the entire restaurant to toast his buddy the groom. Classic New Orleans fun.

Dishes are huge. We split everything and were still too full for more than a bite of dessert. Alligator sausage cheesecake, duck gumbo, caught-that-day-fresh fish. All perfectly cooked and seasoned. The food is exceptional. Southern-style sides were magnificent.

We came as a group of 11 girls in town for a bachlorette party last Saturday night. I was very very pleased.

We arrived early for our reservation, it is about a 15 min cab ride from Canal St..  As I went to get a round of drinks from the bar the hostess found me and told me our table was already ready.
The tablecloths are a mixmash of colorful oilcloths and are festive, the murals on the walls are pretty and unique, personal touches everywhere!
Our servers were great.  The food prices were a little higher than expected but everything is large enough to share. 

Everyone was really happy with the items we tried- 
Watermelon Mojito- Fresh watermelon and mint, light, refreshing, YUM!
Cornbread muffins- garlicky & buttery goodness
Alligator Cheesecake- most were reluctant to try but I thought it was great! A quiche consistancy with a great cream sauce.
BBQ Shrimp (beware, they still have their heads if that kind of thing ookes you out) but the buttery, garlicky, rice underneth is heavenly.
Fried Roast Beef Poboy- Was massive! 
Fried Chicken- Heard audible "yummmms" from that end of the table, two of them splitting it couldn't even finish off the plate.
My favorite side was the corn (apologies it does have a fancier name.)

Chef Jack came and welcomed us then later sent a complimentary round of champagne for the bride. Call me a dork, I love this kind of personal service.

We were loud but fit right in and from beginnng to end everyone left with a smile on their face. We went nextdoor to the Maple Leaf to join our new found restaurant friends and a good time was had by all.

My bachelorette brunch was absolutely amazing! The food was delicious and worth every penny. Our waitress was very fun and made great recommendations. We had a pretty big party but everything flowed smoothly and our food came out within a reasonable time. The French Toast Batons tasted like heaven on a plate! Whenever I'm back in New Orleans, this will be a definite stop. The only recommendation I'd make is to use the online reservation app to reserve a spot before you arrive. Through the app, our wait was 45 minutes and we were able to walk in once we arrived.

This is a fun place. They do a clapping routine for people's birthdays which is way less embarrassing than singing and gets everyone in the restaurant involved. Food is very good. Can't beat the boozy coffee and eggs Benedict menu. Great service and great atmosphere.

On Friday 24th of May, 2019 we started hubby's birthday celebration on our trip in New Orleans, Louisiana with Brunch at The Ruby Slipper, which he chose. This place is very popular so make sure you join the wait list before strolling through the streets to this establishment, which we did. Hubby ordered The Trifecta- 3 different style Eggs Benedict. One serving each of the Egg Conchon (apple braised pulled pork), Chicken St. Charles (fried chicken) and Shrimp Boogaloo (creole style tomato sauce) Benedict. They were served on freshly baked Buttermilk Biscuits. The eggs were perfectly poached and Aaron loved the entire dish. I ordered the Hot Smoked Salmon Omelette with Goats cheese, topped with dill, hollandaise, red onion and flash fried capers. My meal was absolutely delicious and I will say the small piece of Buttermilk biscuit that I tried was the best I've ever had. It was soft and not dense at all, in saying that I'm not a huge biscuit fan. You can order potatoes or fruit as your side with this dish too. Bloody Mary's and Mimosas are very popular over here when enjoying a Brunch. I order the Ruby Slipper Big Easy Mimosa which has OJ and a dash of Pomegranate added to the champagne. I must say this is the best mimosa I've had yet since moving to the States. They were not wrong when they said easy pour mimosa either! We sat at the bar so I watched them being made. A large wine glass 3/4 full with champagne then topped with juice for $10. Hubby had the Vodka based Bacon infused Bloody Mary which also was amazing! Overall the staff were fabulous, friendly and very accommodating on a usual busy long weekend Brunch morning. The menu has lots of choices and the meals were a fantastic price. A great birthday brunch was had and my husbands iced coffee on a very warm NOLA day to go was also very good to top it off!

This restaurant was great for our group of 10 who were in town for bachelor party weekend. Everyone's food was delicious and prices were reasonable. Waitress had no problem cashing us out at a timely manner and was very friendly might I add.

This restaurant was great for our group of 10 who were in town for bachelor party weekend. Everyone's food was delicious and prices were reasonable. Waitress had no problem cashing us out at a timely manner and was very friendly might I add.

We went to the courtyard to celebrate our 50th anniversary - and after stopping in the night before, we were looking forward to a nice FUN evening.  The very nice man told us just to come on down and they would take care of us.  Well, apparently our "man" was not on duty on Saturday.  We arrived at the courtyard, and the girl finally noticed we were waiting at her podium - after all she was giggling with several young fellow workers at the time.  She then seated us right outside the kitchen doors - felt the breeze of bodies going past with their trays of food.  When asked for a different spot she said that maybe in 15 minutes - sit along the side to wait.  We decided to sit inside the little bar instead - very noisy but a table nonetheless.  Charlie was our server and within minutes made us feel welcome.  The beer was served in cans, no glasses -and my Pimms cup was very good. We ordered Shrimp Jumblya and crawfish ettufie (pardon the spelling) - both dishes were very GOOD - feeling better about our choice of celebration locations. Then Charlie brought us a marvelous bread pudding dessert with two spoons - oh, my! Very good.  After we ate, we wandered to the piano bar, but soon left to find some Jazz.  Would recommend the food here - and young clueless staff aside, a good spot.  Thanks, Charlie!  You saved the evening.

I've had a really fun time here on many occasions. I came with my mom and my husband one winter when it wasn't too busy. The music was fun but we could talk in the courtyard. When we came with my dad here, it was crowded but still fun. We got drinks and amazing fried alligator appetizers. 

When I came here for an event, it was an amazing time. My strawberry daiquiri was very tasty and strong and I was able to order at the bar while watching the pianist play wonderfully executed live covers. 

Come here to party and you'll get great service but the best is when you're there for a corporate event.

Pretty cool bar just off of bourbon street.  They have a few different rooms such as their main bar (which was dead), the back patio (which was bumping with drunkards soaking up the sun), and your dimly lit piano bar which had two ladies bumping requested tunes.  

Do order their Hurricane here.  It will knock your socks off.
Don't order anything else besides that or beer.  I ordered an old-fashioned (wtf was I thinking?) and the bartender threw some crap together that reminded me of college.  He even used his bare hands to rip the orange meat off an orange slice just for the peel.  I just hoped he washed his hands ...

Anyways, the dueling pianos was a blast.  The crowd was mixed, including your bachelorette party, older seniors slamming down a few hurricanes, drunk frat boys pounding beers, and your standard 30's people like my wife and I.  However, everyone was having a super fun time which was awesome.

Bathrooms are really dingy but what do you expect?

4 stars!

GREAT food for a drunken fix! 
Love ZACHARY- he's the bar tender that served my bachelorette party! He was super kind, polite, and attentive. Thanks for a great experience. 
recommend the GUMBO! They serve it with okra which was so Delish!

results.question: cheap brunch place with outdoor seating
results.answer: The Country Club is a great option for a cheap brunch with bottomless mimosas and outdoor seating. It's especially fun and lively on weekends. Ruby Slipper - New Orleans is another solid choice, especially on weekdays when you can walk right in. For a unique experience, Tableau offers a charming antique balcony with bottomless mimosas and a beautiful view, though it might be a bit pricier.
contexts:
$10 bottomless mimosas on the weekends!!!! That is really all you need to know!!

Food = Yummy. I especially enjoy the fried green tomato benedict. 
Mimosas = Flowing. definitely more champagne than OJ in that mixture. You take one sip and they refill your glass, now that's what I call service! 
Atmosphere = Fun. Everyone is here to have a good time. Make a reservation, it gets super crowded. 
Service = See Mimosas. Excellent.

I have never been to the pool, always too full after brunch. Maybe one day....! Separate review to come once I do.
Just, awesome. Bottomless mimosas on Sunday is great, and a great waitstaff.

We had the pleasure of dining in at The Country Club for Sunday breakfast. We made reservations in advance (highly recommend doing the same) and were seated at our table in no time. Heather took wonderful care of us and we did not once see the bottom of our mimosa glasses!!! She even took us to the pool after we ate to check it out. We're so thankful of her service and how amazing everything was. Definitely the highlight of our food we had over the weekend!
Weekends u may wait an hour for a table, plan for it, have a mimosa.  During the week, Monday to Friday , walk rite in and have a wonderful breakfast.  Very good place for breakfast.

Full disclosure: Sat on the antiques balcony and drank bottomless mimosas on a beautiful day. 

On my next rendezvous with Posh Beckham, this is where we'll imbibe. On the antiquey balcony overlooking strolling pedestrians amongst grandiose oaks lined across the stone boulevard, is where we savagely slurped mimosas with our delectable plates of grits, eggs, burgers, and crepe brûlée. 

Come, be carefree, enjoy the perfect service and view, take in the glory that is a meal well eaten and time well spent.

Made reservations through OpenTable. Arrived and it was busy weekend so we were seated at bar (party of two). Mimosas for drinks and cool part is we could see through the receiving window and watch our food cooked. Energy was upbeat and food was delicious

The menu here is amazing.  I had the Eggs Cochon which was a little strong but tasty.  The biscuit was not overcooked and the eggs were perfect. Each portion of the dish stood on its own.  On weekends,  it seems an employee is posted near the hostess to provide mimosas and other brunch cocktails to waiting customers.   The cocktails were mostly sparkling wine with little to no mixer and SERVED IN Dixie cups.   I'm paying $60 for bunch, your mimosa should be served in a flute.  Servers are a little slow.  The seating area is clean and well-lit.

Enjoyed a nice breakfast here one morning. We were staying at the Roosevelt so it was a very close walk from the hotel.  It is a pretty popular breakfast spot, especially on the weekends so we had to wait 30 minutes for the table but luckily it was a gorgeous day so we didn't mind the wait.  Once seated, we ordered our drinks.....it was brunch on a Sunday so there is nothing better than a mimosa.  What I especially liked about the mimosa's here are that there was 4 different options with various juices.  I chose the mimosa with fresh berry juice, it was good!  I also ordered the french toast which was your standard french toast, nothing amazing however still good.

results.question: jazz brunch with live music
results.answer: Tableau is a great spot for jazz brunch with live music, offering a fun atmosphere and delicious food. The live band that plays at your table is a special touch, and the drinks are a highlight. Atchafalaya Restaurant also offers a fantastic jazz brunch experience, with a cool ambiance and excellent service, though the food might not be as outstanding. Buffa's Bar & Restaurant is another excellent choice, known for its lively female-led jazz band and affordable, tasty brunch dishes.
contexts: Can't leave without trying out Tableau for brunch. Live band coming around the table is an extra touch. Drinks were the highlight. #hellohelloeats #eattheworld #foodporn #musteat #livetoeat #eatswithlovedones #withmybb#icjourneys #eatwithcin #flexitarian #ourchoice #NOLA
Jazz brunch was a treat! Bottomless mimosas table side band that plays tunes by request with delicious food! What more could a New Orleans gal ask for??!

One of the best meals we had in New Orleans. Brunch on Saturday included live jazz going from table to table. Food was excellent, seafood gumbo served with baguette was great, followed by amazing and fresh main courses, nothing overcooked or under-spiced--tasty. Fun cocktails as well as mimosas to choose from. Had to ask for water and lemon twice, but other than that, they were very friendly. I would go here again and would recommend it.

Super cool ambiance for brunch with live jazz. Great service and really liked the vibe of the place. The food was good, but didn't blow me away. I got the chicken and biscuits, which was nothing special. The build your own bloody bar is a nice touch.

Great brunch experience with the parents.
Live jazz music downstairs. Was great music but made it too loud to sit in the front room, so be sure to ask to be seated in the upstairs room.

Eggs Atchafalaya and Eggs Louisianne are both great. Grits and Grillades qerw great too, with surprisingly large chunks of meat (just a bit too much black pepper added at the end to the meat though).

Great service. Excellent bloody mary bar. (but the bm mix seemed a bit thin).

I will definitely be back!

I came here for brunch and it was great, the food was good, but perhaps equally as important was the atmosphere, which included a live band playing jazzy tunes. i wasn't too hungry so I just got the fruit parfait, but I also sampled some other peoples' food which was yummy as well.

My favorite place
Jazz brunch was the tits and so
Was the food and drinks

Love this neighborhood spot for its jazz brunch. The female-led band knows their stuff. Each music set is lively and fun. The brunch menu offers basic breakfast dishes, which are done well. I had eggs, hashbrowns, bacon and grits --  all good. The eggs Benedict with homefries was good as well. And $5 bloody Marys are the perfect complement to the meal and ambiance.

Come to jazz brunch with at least 2 hours- it's packed, but the music and atmosphere is worth it!

This Sunday morning, there were 2 super women serving this back room with red walls and a little stage. These women hostesses, bartended, and served a room of 50+ guests. I hope Buffa's owner considers giving these lovely women a break, and giving customers a chance to eat in less than 2 hours.

The food is very cheap, and very okay. For $9, i had a tasty homemade biscuit with 2 poached eggs and hash browns. The hash browns serving was very crispy and very small. Coffee is solid and only $1.5.

The music was excellent. An 8 piece jazz band that has such a lovely flair. 

Pro tip: come in after 1pm. The band was still playing and the place is much less crowded.

Live Oak has become my favorite brunch place Uptown.  The huevos rancheros are what keep me coming back, but I am a big fan of their creamy, cheesy grits too.  I also appreciate that they accommodate those on a vegetarian diet by subbing avocado for meat in their dishes.  Their coffee, from French Truck, is top notch too.  The staff all seem to be hardworking, fun people who check on the customers regularly, especially to make sure said coffee is refilled.  Grant waited on us the last time and made us laugh.  And of course there's the live music, which is an added bonus at brunch.

results.question: late-night Cajun food after a show on Frenchmen Street
results.answer:
Atchafalaya Restaurant is a top choice for late-night Cajun food, especially if you're looking for a unique dining experience. The chicken andouille gumbo and pasta atchafalaya are highly recommended, though be prepared for a slightly higher price point. 

Buffa's Bar & Restaurant offers authentic Cajun food with a live music scene and a 24-hour kitchen, making it perfect for a late-night meal. The atmosphere is lively, and the staff is friendly, though the place can be a bit snobby about its wine selection.

The Original Pierre Maspero's is another great option, especially if you're in the French Quarter. The blackened jambalaya and the BBQ shrimp appetizer are standout dishes, and the place offers a unique atmosphere with its historic setting and tourist-filled streets.
contexts: Yelp directed me to this as one of the best cajun places open late on a Sunday night. Good call yelp. Live music, darkly lit, very cool restaurant. The chicken andouille gumbo and pasta atchafalaya were pretty awesome. Loses one star for being a bit too snobby with the wine. Not a glass for under $11. But otherwise great place.

This restaurant was my introduction to Cajun/Creole food and it rocked my world. I loved the shrimp and grits and the fried green tomatoes. It's a charming little place a couple of blocks from Magazine street; the ambiance was just lovely, the hostess/restaurant owner was incredibly friendly and helpful and the amazing food just kept coming.

This was the first meal I had in New Orleans during my weekend trip and it set the tone perfectly for the food adventures to come. I've always loved New Orleans style cooking but damn, the people here just blow my mind. Don't think I'll ever eat Cajun anywhere else again. 

Came for brunch. Parking sucks, but that's not unique to Atchafalaya. We ordered: grapefruit Bellini, shrimp and grits, chicken & biscuits, duck hash, and the étouffée omelet. We had "an hour" wait but was more like 20 mins. 

Everything was delicious, but things could've been better. Duck hash barely had duck, the étouffée omelet fell a little flat in flavor, the chicken and biscuits were divine (biscuits are the PERFECT amount of sweetness and fluff), and the shrimp and grits were above average, though I've had better. I would say omelet and chicken and biscuits were the better two dishes, but over all still an amazing meal!

Qualms: the price is a little steep compared to some nearby places, and the place is a little tiny. If you're cool with that then this would probably be the perfect place to come for a bougier but unarguably delicious meal.

Oh Yelp, you've helped me hunt down a new, amazing place once again. While staying in the Garden District, I was riding the trolly back from the Quarter. I saw this restaurant with a big patio and a wall of windows, and great reviews on yelp. I hopped off the trolly and got seated immediately. The restaurant is beautiful! High ceilings and a big bar with lots of wooden accents.

The food is creative with a Cajun New Orleans flair. The crawfish mac & cheese is loaded with the mudbugs, then topped with more cheese and crispy bread crumbs. The salads are massive! The side salad here is the size of a regular salad at any other restaurant. The seafood tastes really clean and fresh, but my favorite dish is the rabbit braised with white beans. It's a total comfort food dish, like a hug in a bowl. 

I also really enjoyed all the cocktails I sampled. The place is also a distillery, so the vodka and gin are made in house. As you sit down to eat, you can see all the stills and fermenters in the back.

This Cajun-Southern restaurant is in the French Quarter and was suggested to us by a friend who had an amazing meal here and boy did they not disappoint.  The food here is farm-to-table and is at a price point anyone can afford. You can BYOB at this place too which saves you a bit if you plan to drink. One thing to remember when going here, talk to your waiter.  They can direct you to what will be best for you on the menu based on what you are feeling.  The Chicken Fried Chicken is amazing so filling and hearty and if you're in the mood for some smoked salmon, cream cheese, and a bagel the Lox and Bagel is the way to go (comes with a good bit of fruit too).

Cozy restaurant on the corner that feels a little like a converted ground-floor apartment with nice nooks for seating. Solid cajun food with nice range of specials that left me just enough to choose from without feeling overwhelmed. Fried okra was great, as was the salmon & dill special. 

Quick note: this place doesn't have a bar, so make sure to grab some beer or wine at the convenience store a block away before you head in!

This gets 5 out of 5 for the following reasons:
-Authentic cajun food at a reasonable price matched with reasonable quality
-Authentic New Orleans establishment that has been around for many many years
-Staff & owner are awesome human beings
-Live music in the back room!
-24hr kitchen!
-Two different bars, depending on your mood. The corner entrance is a smokey dive that allows dogs and you can still order from the kitchen. The back room (not 24hrs) is sit down dining, smoke free, and often has good, local live entertainment

When friends visit I will always take them to buffa's. There may be better "fine dining" in NOLA and arguable better cajun food, but the mix of decent food, amazing new orleans atmosphere and 24hr service makes this place a must.

Woot. Decently priced Cajun and Creole food in the heart of the Quarter with no wait on a Saturday night? Awesometown! 

Our server was a sweetheart. She was friendly, informed and prompt. The red beans and rice were bomb! My bf had chicken parmesan and it was southern fried chicken parmesan with angel hair pasta and so tasty!

Will definitely return here. If you can get a window seat, ask for one so you can watch all the people walking by. =)

The original Pierre Maspero's is a great location to sampled some of New Orleans Cajun cuisine in the Big Easy.

It has large doors that open onto the tourist filled streets of the French Quaters. It is in an older building like much of the downtown area, with brick walls and gaslighting. There is an older handcrafted wooden staircase next to the bar which leads to the second floor, it is unique and shows how the structures were constructed in the olden days. 

Check in using your Yelp app and you will likely get a free appetizer with the order of two entrees.

We try the BBQ shrimp appetizer shrimp served in a Buttery Cajun sauce and some bread. It was very tasty. This was a 13 or $14 appetizer which was free after checking in to Yelp. What a deal!

The blacken jambalaya was excellent and hit the spot. It was not too spicy, seasoned just right. The BLT chicken sandwich had a different spice on it... perhaps rosemary and was not as tasty as the blackened chicken.

Overall this is a great place To enjoy lunch or dinner in the French Quarters, relax your feet and enjoy the sites.

For a beer selection we tried Abita the boot which was light crisp and tasty. The large size is 24 oz and only $2 more than the small.

We just stumbled upon really good Cajun food and cocktails in the heart of the French quarter! We ordered Cajun blackened chicken with fettuccine Alfredo and blackened shrimp jambalaya! I had the perfect storm cocktail and my husband had the hurricane. Shelly was our waitress and she was a hoot and really quick to get you what we needed even though it was a packed house!

results.question: family-friendly seafood place with parking
results.answer: Jimmy J's Cafe offers a cozy and family-friendly dining experience with a good variety of seafood options. The place is well-paced, serving a broad menu efficiently. Deanie's Seafood, while not the top pick, is a spacious and friendly spot with a casual atmosphere, offering huge fried seafood platters and a great selection of southern dishes. Both places have good reviews for their family-friendly environments and ample seating, though Deanie's might be a bit more hit-or-miss depending on the day.
contexts: Cozy place. They need to turn the 9 or so tables over pretty quick in order to make money.  They do a good job of it. You'd think a smaller menu would help them do that but oh no. Their menu is pretty broad. Had the poboy with oyster and shrimp. It was good. Wife had blueberry French toast and daughter had bananas foster French toast.  Son had eggs and bacon with home fries. Also we had bacon and it was good. Ambience was nice and art was cool.

After getting lots of recommendations for this place, we came for dinner at the end of a long day from a plantation tour.  Deanie's is what we in New England would consider a "clam shack".  Its a casual place that sells basic seafood (lots of fried stuff).  They have some great beers on tap and they're served to you ice cold. Instead of a bread basket, you get a dish of boiled red potatoes seasoned with the seafood broth. I found that a bit odd, considering you get french fries with most of their dinners. There broiled red fish was great.  The fried seafood platters are HUGE!!  They offer a 1/2 size seafood platter, but that is still HUGE!!  I wasn't a huge fan of the batter they use on their fried seafood, but I didn't hate it.  The waitstaff here is really quite friendly.  I would recommend Deanie's to people.

My FAV seafood (FRIED PLATTER-wise) in the French Quarter area. This place is spacious and full of southern hospitality. The wait is usually up there for a table (nothing like those long drawn out waits at Acme) unless you time it correctly.

The staff are true Nawlins and I LOVE IT from the Southern twang to the way they celebrate life like there is no tomorrow. 

We almost always get the same items
- Crab Nachos
- BBQ Shrimp
- 1/2 seafood platter 

The potatoes that come out before the food is amazing. I warn you "Don't eat too many as you won't be able to finish your meal if you do". The potatoes are so fresh and tasty with the butter.
Crab nachos are good but pretty much a heart attack on a platter due to all be cheese and such added. I tend to only take a bite or so to say I had it. The BBQ shrimp is extremely messy you must prepare to get messy. The 1/2 seafood platter is the HIGHLIGHT of Deanies (most people get the whole but I don't suggest this unless you are with a group of 4 or more). This platter screams flavor filled with various seafoods and tasty treats l like shrimp, scallops, fish, hush puppies, corn, etc..

A must do every visit to NawLins ;-)

After searching the Quarter for good, reasonably priced meals for 20 years now I think we found a gem in Deanie's Seafood! Restaurant was hopping on a Wednesday night (good sign)-20 minute wait for table. I think I had the BEST crawfish etoufee ever-and I can make a good one. Dining partner's gumbo was also really tasty--nice, thick roux--puts Acme's & Felix's to shame.  Those cute little (spicy!) (free) potatoes they serve as an "appetizer" are good, too! We didn't try the oysters but heard they get theirs from the same supplier as Acme & Felix's so they're probably great, too.Fried seafood platters were HUGE!!  Nice big bar--packed with people. Looked like mostly locals---as they've been in business in Bucktown for a long time.  Walked away fat and happy and still had $$ in our pockets--not bad!

This restaurant saved my NOLA vacation amidst a sea of subpar restaurants in the Frwnch Quarter touting 4 star reviews from tourists who can't possibly have pallettes. Everything about this restaurant was excellent; atmosphere, service, food, portions, and price. 

Atmosphere: has a "grandma's dining room" vibe to it. Real homey and warm. Great for a family meal, hangout with friends, or a date.

Food: The food was all top quality. The seafood gumbo and turtle soup were rich and had a home-cooked feel to them. Gumbo is everywhere down here, which means mediocre gumbo is abundant, and this was definitely a cut above the rest. Solid. 

I got the gulf fish. The fish itself was excellent. Crispy, light, and very rich in flavor, from what I think was a wine-based sauce, but not sure. $20 was a great price for this. It came with a side of fries, but they were out of place and didn't complement the fish well. The fish was good enough to overshadow that.

My girlfriend got the shrimp pasta. Excellent dish. The portion size was huge; both for the pasta and the actual shrimp, so no skimping on anything (as opposed to restaurants that put a pound of pasta and 4 shrimp in a dish and them call it Seafood Pasta). And the sauce - not sure was it was, but oil based - was very light and flavorful. 

Lastly, the Milkyway Pie. Christ. I thought it would taste like sugar crack covered in caramel goo that you'd get from a deli fridge, but I was wrong. It was more like a chocolate mousse on top of a brownie/cookie crust. Just get it. 

This is definitely one of my top restaurants in NOLA.

Seafood restaurant on Bourbon St.  Fast friendly service. This was a spur of the moment choice, the alcohol and tireless walking had set it. The restaurant was packed, but we were sat rather quickly.

Chicken and Sausage gumbo- it had a very nice texture to the soup- not too watery and not too thick like gravy. The pepper overtones kind of took over the dish, but overall quite satisfying. 

Oysters, bacon bits, and cheese. - was a bit of disappointment. On the dry side. Hardly any bacon on the oysters. C-minus at best. 

Overall it's a Good place or a quick bite.

It is a very clean, modern and spacious restaurant. Came in a group for lunch, service was very attentive, it seems they have good alcoholic drinks but I cannot drink any of those :-( We ordered a bunch of items from their lunch menu: lobster Mac n cheese, grilled gulf fish, chicken sandwiches. Food came out quickly and together for all the people which is amazing! My gulf fish tastes ok, not too impressive, I mean, the fish is really thin and seems to be over-grilled, and the rice is basically soaked in olive oil for some reason. The grilled veggies are really good though. My husband likes the chicken sandwiches also. Other friends said their lobster Mac N cheese are good, even though the lobsters are too small to locate lol. Generally a nice place to hung out. Nice environment, ok food, and reasonable prices (each lunch item entree is only around 15 bucks).

results.question: quiet romantic date spot
results.answer:
The Tasting Room is a fantastic choice for a quiet romantic date night. The perfect atmosphere, great wine and cheese selections, and attentive service make it ideal for a special evening. 

Chappy's Restaurant offers a charming outdoor setting that feels like you're dining under the stars. The spiced shrimp and impeccable service make it a perfect spot for a romantic dinner. 

Jamila's Mediterranean Tunisian Cuisine is highly recommended for its delightful date night atmosphere and delicious food. The personal service and delectable dishes like the shrimp and mussels tarragon pasta make it a standout option.
contexts: Perfect date night atmosphere with great wine and cheese.  Every one of the staff members thanked us for coming and welcomed us back. We will definitely return for future date nights.

Perfect place for afternoon girl talk or date-night! 

Friends of mine were just finishing up a youth biology summit where they had been dealing with tons of pesky  teenage girls so I assume there was no other thing they wanted but wine. I figured The Tasting Room was the perfect place. 

Being held hostage in a college dormitory all weekend...the gorgeous chandeliers, modern furniture and cute and serene courtyard were a good change of scenery. 

We ordered a mix of flights....reds, whites, and bubbly's which range between $20-$25. Great selections and the waiters give you tons of info on what pairs well together. Also, they have pre-selected flights if you are a bit confused about what to try. The charcuterie plate was pre-selected as well with cheeses and meats which were great choices. 

The food menu (although I didn't try anything this time around) seems pretty awesome and changes with whatever is currently in season. 

Great addition to your afternoon drink spots.

Love the newly renovated location. The management and bar are all super welcoming. 
We didn't try food but we did try the $6 chambongs. Definitely worth the price. The patio area is really cool..even though the weather is super moody. 
I would highly recommend here for a date night, gurls bight, bachelorette weekend and any other event involving twirls and a good time!!
The ambiance and service (including the jovial owner and his son) provided the perfect date night environment! Delicious food too! Definitely going back soon!

Such a wonderful experience! The food, service and atmosphere were delightful. We got the shrimp and mussels tarragon pasta, salade tunisienne and couscous royale. All of them were delicious, but the personal service is what made this place so special. Great date night! Can't wait to go back

Cute restaurant tucked at the end of magazine.  You walk in and you feel like you're sitting at an outdoor patio underneath the stars.  Entrees come with choice of salad or soup with a side.  Great price.  Had the spiced shrimp that came with jambalaya and a crab cake on top.  Delicious and perfectly spiced.  Service was impeccable and attentive.  Perfect date night spot.

This was the best date night we've ever had...we did the cooking competition on a Saturday night and the moment we walked in we felt super comfortable yet so excited! We made so many new friends, got to learn a lot of really game changing cooking tips from Chef Mel, and for $60/person - we were full off of amazing food! (first glass of wine is free too)! We will definitely be going back.
Fantastic and fun evening.  Excellent wine pairings.  Go!  You will not be disappointed.

We returned to Langlois for another fabulous date night!   Interactive dining at its best!    We have been here several times and each visit is better than the last.   The staff is attentive and courteous, anticipating our needs but not intrusive at all.   We are almost on a first name basis with everyone there!   Each time we visit,  our participation in the food preparation is different and we are always pleasantly surprised with how much we learn.   We love that the menu is seasonal and,  being a farmer's daughter,  the farm to table menu suits me perfectly.   Very inexpensive for a five or six course dinner (I lost count) along with wine pairings and bubbly!   Thanks, Chef Amy and Chef Tess, for a delightful time!  We'll be back!
Had cocktails at the bar prior to Xmas Eve Dinner at G.W. Finns. Sam the bartender was fantastic and we trusted his judgment on everything. Ask for The Hibiscus.

results.question: outdoor patio restaurant with a full bar
results.answer: Copper Monkey Grill offers a cozy and intimate outdoor dining experience on its small, enclosed patio. The place is perfect for a quiet dinner, and the staff is very respectful of the patrons' privacy. Twelve Mile Limit has a great atmosphere and a friendly crowd, with its outdoor patio providing a cozy vibe. While Mid-City Yacht Club has a spacious and comfortable outdoor area with a fountain and fans, it might be a bit too touristy for a full bar experience, though it's definitely a place to consider for a different vibe.
contexts:
Let me note, before I start my review, that, as is true of all of my reviews, this review is only for the food and service, and for the liquor, since I do not drink alcoholic drinks.

This is a great little pub on Conti, a few doors down from Bourbon Street. The food is great, the service is great, and the interior is nice: a very comfortable old French Quarter pub with no pretenses. I have eaten a quiet dinner of a big juicy custom-made hamburger many times at this pub during my stays in the Quarter.

Whenever I am eating dinner here, I almost always ask to be seated at one of the two small tables located in the tiny outdoor patio near the rear of the bar area, Now, when I say "outdoor patio", let me explain that the term "outdoor" means simply that it is open to the sky above. The patio is very small, and is fully enclosed on a all sides by buildings and building walls, and is largely used by the restaurant for storage of some cleaning supplies, but the little patio is very cozy, very cute, and a perfect place for one or two persons (or perhaps three or four if you wanted to push the envelope a bit...) to eat a quite dinner in a great little enclosed outdoor space. And yes, during rainstorms, despite all the surrounding buildgins and stairwells, some of the rain does reach the little patio, at least the portions of the patio that are not shielded by overhead balconies or stairwells. 

I have NEVER encountered any other customers when I have eaten my dinners on this patio; I have always had it all to myself, and all of the staff, including both bar waitstaff and the kitchen staff, seem to appreciate what a beautiful little secluded place this is, and they are always very respectful of my privacy when I am eating there, and yet they also manage to be very solicitous of my needs, and ask if I might need anything else with my meal. 

So, this tiny outdoor patio is a very well-kept secret! And, it is not for everyone! It is tiny, there is old equipment stored along its edges, and it is in a beautiful state of mild disrepair, and, as you would expect with any really good dive bar, there is something a bit shabby about the patio. In fact, I cannot believe that location scouts for Hollywood studios do not have both the bar itself, and particularly this little outdoor dining patio, booked six months out of the year for "atmospheric" film shoots!

BTW, their burgers are very good, as is all of their food!
Small bar - great atmosphere and great bartenders. Always a friendly crowd. The outdoor patio has a cozy vibe!

When I think of bars, I can't help but think of loud music and obnoxious people. Why can't there be a bar that's chill with space to move, music that's fun and not too loud so you can actually have a conversation? Oh wait! That's The Bulldog Mid City! 
I completely enjoyed the outdoor patio even in the drizzling rain. Gabe and I went here after our dinner at Luke at his suggestion. He was a regular here awhile back and always talked about how I might like it. I loved it! Great old school music and such a variety too. The crowd was in the 30-something range.... late 30's and older maybe. 
What I usually hate about bars is how cramped and stuffy they can be. The outdoor patio gives us claustrophobs a place to not freak out while looking for a stool near the exit. 
The canopy of trees with lanterns and string of lights are a nice touch. Definitely look forward to going back.   
I couldn't think of a reason not to give it 5 stars. 
Great music, beer, atmosphere and company!

I have been coming here often for years. Love this bar! It is always fun with a date or with a group of friends. The seating area in the back outdoor patio is nice on a cool night. They have a large selection of beers on tap. The food and the service is top-notch as well. I suggest going for their trivia night. It's always a good time.

Visited the Bulldog Mid-city twice in the last year. I really like the patio mostly because it is DOG FRIENDLY. No waitress service for food or drinks. If it's crowded expect to wait at the bar for drinks and sometimes the kitchen gets overwhelmed and closes when they get behind. WAY TOO LOUD INSIDE TO HAVE PLEASANT CONVERSATION. 

Once you get situated the patio is very nice. TVs around to watch the game and the draft beer selection is great. I agree with other reviewers that the beer is overpriced especially considering the smaller sized pitchers used which contain about four beers. The nachos were reasonably priced and very good. We'll be back........

DOG FRIENDLY ON THE PATIO.

The atmosphere is not like a traditional bar !
The outside patio is very spacious and has a fountain. To keep the patio guest cool there're many fans and misters making things very conferrable
The foods offered are full of different variety.
We stopped to have drinks and a bite with friends while on vacation . this first visit was a great experience  and we will be back .

Cool LOCALS bar with awesome AC inside in addition to an outdoor patio. 

16 taps and plenty more choices in the bottle and can. 

We got Yachtchos (nachos) as snack. Fresh made chips and habanero crema really hit the spot.

Been here over a dozen times, I love the vibe and the staff. Inside is always fun and lively, whereas the back patio is a bit more toned down and relaxing. You can sit outside under the lights and just hang out or go inside and get a little loud. They have pool, darts, and some egambling as well. Love this bar and love its location!

Ok, I *know* this is a tourist trap. But whatever, I love it. The outdoor patio is absolutely heaven on a beautiful night. The drinks are big and delicious, the atmosphere is energetic but cheerful and comfortable, and people are just happy here! One night we were here we met an awesome couple from Penn. and sat chatting with them like old friends for quite a while before moving on to another location. That's the type of place it is. My only minor complaint is that it is kind of annoying that they automatically charge you for the glass when you order one of their special drinks (the hurricane, the rainstorm, etc.). Yes, I know most people will take home at least one glass, but then it's annoying to have to bring ones you don't want over to the bar to get refunded for the extra charge.

results.question: dog-friendly restaurant with a patio
results.answer: Barracuda offers a cute outdoor dining experience with a dog-friendly back patio, though the food might be a bit hit-or-miss with its basic taco offerings. Chais Delachaise has a cozy and delicious patio, with a special dish to try and a warm, dog-friendly atmosphere. LUCA eats boasts a stunning and quiet patio that's perfect for enjoying a meal with your furry friend, and the food is fresh and delicious.
contexts: You order at a window up front then sit and wait out back - outdoor dining only. The back patio area is cute with picnic tables. It's a little too cold out now for eating outside, even though they do have heat lamps. The place is very dog friendly - there were three large dogs at other tables while I was there. The menu doesn't have much variety- just a few different tacos, and I thought they were just ok, don't really have much seasoning or flavor. but there is a mushroom taco if you don't eat meat. It's a nice ambiance for warmer days.

I love this place! The ambience is cozy, the food is delicious, and they have a great patio. I have had the pleasure in speaking to one of the owners in my visits and she's very hospitable. One of my favorite dishes is the crab and pepper salad. It's fresh and delicious. It's a special so grab it when you can! If you just want to go for some drinks and small plates, it's a great option. If you want an artfully crafted sit-down meal? Go for it. And the patio is dog-friendly, so bring your fur-kids!

This restaurant is under new management. It is clean, and the food is really good and the staff is super friendly. The restaurant is dog friendly, even have dog bowl.  The place has a two courtyards and they had live music in one of them. One of the courtyards has a ping pong table. I came with wife my five kids and sat in the courtyard, I could have stayed there all day long.

This little place is so special. I stumbled upon it one day and just had to stop by. They have the prettiest patio that's tucked away and quiet. I got the pesto chicken sandwich and it was delicious. I also got a side of fruit and their homemade chips. All I can say is wow. Every single thing was so incredibly fresh. The avocado on my sandwich was abundant and the fruit was so crisp that it must've been cut that morning. 
I will definitely be returning here. 
Also-- their patio is definitely dog friendly and has water bowls for your furry friends.

Love that this restaurant is in a dog friendly hotel and thus dog friendly by virtue. They have seating so you can sit with your pet outside of the restaurant but at the bar, just case someone doesn't want to sit beside a dog. Service is top notch and food is great and reasonably priced. I've had the Benedict's and this last time had the oyster log. Amazing price and delish. You get a total of 18 oysters. So good. I will be back!

If I could give it 6 stars, I would. Reservations usually aren't needed, but on Yelp it said they allowed pets, so I made a reservation through OpenTable and noted that we have a small dog. I received a call from the host who informed me that only applied to service animals, but she offered to set up seating on the patio for us and the dog. The patio had an awning and it rained hard but was still in the 70s. We had the gumbo, the blue crab bisque, a shrimp entree and mahi mahi. For dessert it was beignets and peach cobbler. Everything was spectacular. Our server was the most attentive server I have ever had...which is especially great when you're the only party on the patio and servers sometimes forget about them. Ours was Johnny on the spot. We were on a long trip across the country and had been doing a fair bit of fast food and takeout, so it was nice to have a great dining experience.

results.question: upscale dinner spot that takes reservations
results.answer: For an upscale dinner spot that takes reservations, I recommend Commander's Palace. The reviews highlight its fine dining experience, impeccable service, and beautiful ambiance. Another great option is GW Fins, which offers a luxurious dining experience with attentive service and fresh, creative dishes. While Broussard's is also a nice choice, some reviews suggest it might not fully meet the high expectations set by its 4-star rating, particularly regarding the temperature of the food.
contexts:
No reservation for us. Walked in and enjoyed 3 courses for dinner. I know! How did this happen to people who are not the King/Queen of Zamunda? 

Well, quite frankly, we just ate at the bar. Reservations have to be booked 3 months in advance. There are seats available by the bar. I call them the nursery high chairs. They're great FWIW. I did not mind trying out a new spot and not bathing in white cloths. The appetizers were OHMG!! So GOOD! Crab Beignets and Oysters! The main dish was, Asi Asi (SO SO). I got pasta which was enough for a tween, which I ain't. I'm a woman! LOL! Topped the night off with bread pudding. Not my typical style of bread pudding. Not much of a fan of complicated deserts. K.I.S with deserts. Simple was good enough for Betty Crocker, and my Grandma Shirley so it's good enough for me. Chef's shouldn't need statistics books to make deserts. I will stick with soul food (bread pudding) in this regard.

Wonderful night's plan:
$5 Glass of wine at Patrick's Bar Vin 
Dinner at Restaurant Revolution
Desert at Mr. B's or just go listen to some music in the quarters to burn off those calories. 

Ta Ta for now,
The girl with no reservation

Loved this place. Took my girlfriend to the big easy to enjoy some food , drink and music. Booked a table for two here as the restaurant was in our hotel. We stayed at the hotel royal Sonesta also home of the famous Desire oyster bar. This restaurant is upper class. Staff here are super attentive. For the two of us we had 3 people attending to our needS. One wine sommelier! Death by gumbo, scallops n foi gras , crab beignets, all nicely presented . My girls purse had its own ottoman next to her. They think of everything here. Was a great meal, super service, and Great ambiance! I tipped them fat, all cash. When leaving the head guy ran to us and hooked us up at the jazz club with front table and round of bourbons. It's a dressy place. So dress nice! Some tourists came in Asian I believe with bags off bourbon street and under dressed. Takes away from the whole experience ! Enjoy.. next trip we will go back !! Loved it

Sunday the 23rd we were a party of four
For dinner. Called for reservations and were sat immediately when we arrived . 
Our server was "Story" and she was excellent.  The atmosphere was  elegant ,antique, Orleans with candle light and white table cloths. Just what we were looking for. The menu is small which means every dish is prepared exquisitely. 
The attention to detail, flavor and ingredients was impeccable.  The service was outstanding.  If you plan to dine here, please do it arrive disrespectfully dressed.  No- jackets are not required but no floppy floppies ! This restaurant is worth your time and effort.

Best experience I've had in a long time! 

Let's start with the energy of this restaurant, impeccable! I did make reservations in advance via open table and someone called me 3 hrs prior to arrival to confirm our reservations. It made it feel so personable! When booking your reservations you can state what your visit/special occasion is for. I put down that I was visiting for a date and our waiter was very attentive to our needs and provided us with amazing recommendations. 

The music was great. 
The pace of the waiter, the food, and check was perfect.

Will be returning again!

Tried to get a sat night reservation at 5 pm on a sat night... 10:15 availability.  So, we just delayed and made a 7|30 mon night reservation.   Found it to be a nice space right off bourbon st.  Tables not too close , classy enough, but not over the top classy.  Smoked oysters appetizer yummy.  Both of our fish dishes were vey fresh (drum and halibut) with creative , tasty sauces.    No rush, no hurry, but attentive enough staff.

Notes, I give this a 4 compared to another even better experience at surprisingly  ...  Adolpho's...   on Frenchmen street.

Note, maybe you can get lucky trying to get seats and a meal at the bar without a reservation.  That's just a matter of chance ...otw ..make fri, sat reservations early.

Upscale date night alert !

Make a reservation, dress up, and enjoy a romantic dinner here. I knew what to expect, and The Fin delivers. 


This is a luxury experience convenient to the Bourbon Street shenanigans. We were able to easily walk from the room to this location. We, of course, grabbed a few drinks along the way. 

Thank you for the 5 star experience!

Impeccable service and attention to detail.  Made reservations on OT noting we were in for our anniversary.  Were attended to by excellent waitstaff all around.  A great dinner and experience.  Thank you!

Fine dining elegance in the Garden District. The valet wisks away your car. You are met at the entrance by attentive staff and the dining experience begins. Since we arrived early, the hostess ushered us through the open kitchen to the cozy patio bar. When I passed the Chef's Table, I wanted to plop down on to the leather seats and refuse to leave. Under tropical plants we sipped out drinks until we were led through several dining rooms (there are 7) to a chique 'Garden Room' where the staff continued to amaze and surpass our expectations.
After we ordered, a waitress presented the dessert tray to another table. When someone noticed we stopped to admire the treats, they offered to let us change our order! I kept the bread pudding soufleé, but my husband switched to the praline parfait. All choices are good! 
Ultimate experience!

If you want to experience an impeccably run establishment, look no further. From the moment we called for reservations and I gave my husband's name and they already had the rest of his information, even though I was calling from my cell phone until the moment we left and the valet had our car waiting for us by the time we got downstairs the service was flawless. 

We arrived a bit early so we had pre dinner cocktails in the courtyard. They came and got us when our table was ready. It was nice to be somewhere where people actually dressed for dinner. (You didn't have to be formal, but most men had on a jacket without a tie and most women either a dress or a nice pantsuit.)

The food was fresh and well presented. We wanted to do the seven course tasting menu with pairings, but alas we were driving. That will have to wait for another night.   We had the three course menu.   It was excellent. The highlights were the trio of soups and the bread pudding soufflé. 

Don't miss the Cafe Broulo (sp) if you've never had it. We ended our dinner with it. It was a wonderful coffee drink prepared table side, quite a show, excellent, and to quote our waiter it's supposed to be as dark as night and as sweet as sin. It's an experience not to be missed!  Try it!  You can thank me later.

I'd never been escorted to the ladies room in my life, I kinda liked it.

I called in on Saturday afternoon for dinner reservations for the same evening. Thankfully, we were able to get a reservation for Saturday night  for dinner for 2 at 6:00 PM because they had a cancellation. 

Service is great! Awesome receiving line as you enter the restaurant. They sat us in the Garden Room, which feel like you're sitting in the tree tops. We were able to watch the sun set from up there. It was beautiful.  The staff were super attentive, polite and courteous. I had the gumbo of the day, lamb shank and bread pudding for dessert.  My sister had a side of spinach, fillet Mignon and strawberry shortcake for dessert.  Everything was delicious. 

Save some extra cash if you're going to have dinner here, it's worth it.

Came here for a nice dinner with my wife. If I could give it a 3.5 stars I would. When I came here, this place had 4 stars which is why I chose it, based on the reviews from others. But I don't think it lived up to 4 stars. 

We got there fairly late on a Tuesday night around 8 or so and there were clearly open tables. The hostess asked if we had reservations, which we didn't. She explained almost secretly that they only seat people after they've made reservations, so we stepped out looked them up and made a reservation for 5 mins later on the Open Table App. It wasn't a big deal and at least the hostess was nice enough to let us in on "the secret" but I almost left for that reason.  Just seems to me that you wouldn't want to inconvenience a potential customer? I'm not going to beg you to take my money for an overpriced meal. But maybe they do this so that they get a certain type of customer, ones that are used to making reservations for nicer places to eat? I'm not sure but again seems like a bad business practice. 

So for the actual meal. The dining room was decorated very nicely and it's definitely a classy place, which is good because that's what I wanted for date night with my wife. I ordered a cocktail, Whiskey and Waterloo. It was awesome maybe my favorite cocktail yet. I was undecided, between that and another whiskey cocktail so I asked the waiter and he gave me a good recommendation.  I love crab cakes so we ordered that as an appetizer. It tasted delicious and was very nicely garnished. But it was just barely warm. I would say it felt like just above room temperature. I've worked in a restaurant so I understand that stuff happens, not everything can be piping hot right out of the pan and the wait staff only has so many hands. And at nicer places chefs/cooks don't want their food sitting under heat lamps. So I gave them the benefit of the doubt. I ordered their Pompano Ponchartrain and my wife had the Fillet Mignon. And when they brought out entrees out, the same thing again. Food was only warm. Again both tasted great. If I went back I wouldn't get the pompano again. It was good but nothing incredible. My wife said the Fillet was great, she doesn't get seafood very often so she would probably order it again. 

Overall, nice place, good wait staff, great cocktail, food tasted good but was just barely warm when served. I went with a 4-star expectation and it undelivered just slightly.

results.question: happy hour bar with TVs to watch sports
results.answer: Trenasse is a great spot for watching sports during happy hour. The review mentions enjoying a football game while at the bar, and the oysters and other happy hour specials are highly recommended. Coterie Restaurant & Oyster Bar also offers a fantastic happy hour with great oysters and fresh cocktails, though it's noted as more family-friendly. Both places have a relaxed atmosphere and offer a variety of food and drink options to enjoy while watching the game.
contexts:
Happy Hour is the way to go - fresh delicious oysters, flavorful BBQ shrimp and reasonably priced drinks, Everyday!
We sat at the bar and really enjoyed watching a football game and seeing the bartenders in action.

We stayed at the Intercontinental for 3 nights and came down to the hotel bar for 2 of those days.  The happy hour is from 3-6pm everyday.  The drinks generally range from around $5.  The happy hour drinks offer a pretty wide selection but if you want to try one of the higher end drinks, you'll have to pay full price.  The bartenders are generally very friendly and the atmosphere is relaxed.  There was live music on one of the evenings we were there. 

We had a half dozen chargrilled oysters for $11 and it was delicious.  I would definitely recommend giving it a try.  Overall, a nice place to stop by and have a few drinks.
Boudin balls, debri fries and the smoked wings were AMAZING!! Good for happy hour and not expensive at all! They have the stink juice cocktail which was amazing! The prices are great!

Eat Here!
Drink Here!
See Manager, Jack for great recommendations on everything from drinks to apps
The Garlic Grilled Oysters are Sooo good & fresh!
Shout out to Kara for taking care of us!
Happy Hour is 3-6, $3-$5 for cocktails & beers, but these drinks are so good, you won't mind paying full price.

Great spot for families or date night before a night in Bourbon Street.

We dropped in here by chance and were amazed at the exceptional experience. Josh, the bartender, makes cocktails that are excellently crafted, no mixes, only fresh juices and hand made garnishes, even on French quarter staple drinks like a Bloody Mary or hurricane. Go at happy hour, 3-6 (???) $9 for a dozen of some of the best raw oysters I've had in my life, $5 for cocktails. The overall great experience, the welcoming staff, and the sense of care that goes into every drink and dish yielded a remarkable visit we would recommend to anyone.
Darnell & Wes at the bar are the best! Come here for happy hour during the week for great prices on Oysters & drinks!

We came here to sit on the balcony and for happy hour (daily 2 to 6 pm).  We ordered happy hour small plates (seared yellowfin tuna $6 and filet mignon slider $12) and half priced beer & wine on tap.  The view is great!  Live music spilling up from Jackson Square is even better!

Tableau was great!  We got a table on the balcony during happy hour.  The sweet frites are to die for!  We also had the turtle soup and it was rich. For dessert we went with the best they had which was the sweet dough.  Not too sweet, just right.  Service was spot on and everyone was friendly.  Restrooms were clean.  Highly recommend!

Still love the venue, great balcony (if they will let you sit up there for happy hour which is rare), and probably the best happy hour in the French quarter... Three dollar wines including a Sauvignon blanc, $3 beers (apparently all on tap, haven't sampled), both upstairs and downstairs bar. Upstairs bar recommended as better service from my experiences, but obviously depends upon the bartender on duty.

If they added a healthy salmon or tuna to the menu I would put this next on my list for dinner for sure!

Fun, interactive dinner! Our server Dhavis walked us through the menu, answered our questions, showed us how to use the grill, and really enriched our experience!!! Food was great, drinks were crazy strong! I highly recommend going during happy hour for their version of the long island iced tea ($5 and will get you stumblin'). Asking for Dhavis as your server is a MUST!! He sets the tone and tops off a wonderful meal with a positive, professional, and engaging demeanor!

This is a great place to meet up with friends for happy hour b/c there are a variety of foods and drinks.  Plenty of space and the music was jamming.  The Harvest Moon bourbon cocktail is delicious.  If you like rosemary try this.  Street tacos and pepperoni pizza were perfect for happy hour!

results.question: BYOB place with casual attire
results.answer:
Cleo's Mediterranean Cuisine is a great BYOB place with casual attire. The food is fantastic and the bring-your-own-bottle policy is a plus. You can also enjoy the unique decor and a cool space. 

Satsuma Café offers a cozy and welcoming atmosphere, perfect for working or socializing. It's super cute with an outdoor patio, and the drinks are delicious with a variety of options.
contexts: Super cute & cozy atmosphere! I was looking for a place where I'd be able to set my laptop down for about an hour or so & get some work done...without feeling rushed. The drinks are delicious & I was pleasantly surprised by the number of options.
Not yet overrun by tourists, along with very fresh food and drinks. Cool space, too, with a nice outdoor patio. Very hipster.
I like their juices and the space is welcoming and cozy. The omelette I got was good, but nothing special. Good place to sit on the patio with friends or bring a laptop and get some work done.

The food here is fantastic and we enjoyed the bring your own bottle policy. There is a Kwik mart across canal st that you can go and purchase alcohol at to drink with your meal. I also loved the decor! It didn't feel like New Orleans at all inside .

Our group of 13 was quickly seated despite our short notice reservation. Elliot, our server, was fantastic! She was personable and highly attentive to each of us. The pick-your-bottle of wine option was unique and the food was superb. Flavorful and distinct entrees - swordfish, linguine tritone, boar - are not to be missed. Clean and comfortable atmosphere invites you to take your time amd enjoy the company.
Delicioso! We were able to pick out our own bottle of wine from their vast selection in the back of the restaurant. Amanda was great! Highly suggest. We will be back

This restaurant is so cozy and nice. Went here for my boyfriends birthday. You can choose your own wine then drink it at your table! I thought that was pretty cool. Food was good. Service was really good. Our waiter was super nice and informative about the wine they had

This place is great. Very friendly staff and delicious coffee. If you go to sit for a while to get work done, make sure you bring a jacket, it gets pretty cold in there, which is nice for these hot summer days.

Only complaint is that most of their tables are big round tables that often only has one person sitting at it. having a long community table would be great since the cafe is so popular now!

Really chill vibe with both indoor and outdoor seating. A lot of open space, so it doesn't feel crowded. 

The staff is friendly and attentive. The coffee is also good quality, and there's a good food selection.

Great little place. All the drinks I have tried (regular coffee, ice coffee, chai tea, matcha frap) are great. Staff is friendly. The monkey theme is cute, and they even have a banana shaped pillow on the couch!

Recommended to me by a friend from Buckhead GA (RIP Tom) that said it suited my personality.  After eating there I took it as an compliment.  A relaxed, eclectic atmosphere situated inside a house with an enclosed air conditioned deck that serves as an extra dining area.

They serve your basic rustic French menu but separate themselves from other French restaurants by excelling in the preparation and presentation of the classics and the daily specials with out the pomp and circumstances.... in short: No Formal Wear Required - BUT you can consider this a strong contender for a special evening or date night, and that's a nice combination for a guy who's looking to impress his date without having to dress.

results.question: bachelorette dinner, upscale vibes, good for large groups
results.answer: For an upscale bachelorette dinner with good vibes and suitable for large groups, I highly recommend **Tableau**. The second floor balcony offers a great view and the staff is very accommodating, making it a perfect spot for a celebration. Another excellent choice is **GW Fins**, which provided a memorable experience with fantastic service and delicious food, especially the lobster dumplings and scalibut. Lastly, **Commander's Palace** is also a top pick, known for its southern hospitality, perfect ambiance, and exceptional service, though it might be a bit pricier.
contexts: Went here for a bachelorette party dinner a group of 10. They seated us at beautiful square on the second floor right by a smaller bar. The table was a great option to easily see everyone and have a conversation without having to yell at the table. Staff was welcoming and attentive. They even turned off the ac near us when they saw us shivering. For entrees several of the girls got the Redfish Bienville and raved about it the rest of the weekend. My sister had the steak frites medium and rated that a 3/5 flavor was bland. I had the chicken tableau. The chicken was fall off the bone and a great portion size. For dessert they treated us with the Creme brûlée which was also fantastic. It is a little spendy and would recommend more for a birthday or anniversary setting over a bachelorette party

One of my favorite restaurants in the entire city. From food, service, to atmosphere, this checks it all for me. Ate dinner here for a bachelorette party and they were incredibly accommodating to all 14 of us and did an amazing job making the bride feel celebrated. Really yummy craft cocktails and great plates to share. 

Have the most fun second floor balcony! Hosted a birthday brunch in the green room upstairs, and it was so memorable. The buffet style brunch was fresh and flavorful. I could've bathed in the barbecue shrimp and grits. The jazz band is fantastic, always playing crowd favorites! :)

We had a wonderful time at Tableau while in New Orleans celebrating our friend's bachelorette party. We were a party of 8 *slightly rowdy* females. The staff were amazing and treated us with such kindness and respect. You don't always find that when serving a large party. Our food was delicious and came out timely and hot. Katie Hutchinson, the manager, made sure to stop by and congratulate my friend on her engagement and offered to get the table anything we might need. I would absolutely recommend this restaurant to anyone  and will definitely be back when we visit New Orleans again.

Wow-what can I say?  Honestly, this was the best meal I've had all summer! (and I haven't exactly been dieting...)
Came here for my bachelorette party/dinner with some great friends and we were all very impressed and happy with our choice to come here!
I guess I should start with the service-It was wonderful.  We felt so welcomed and relaxed : ) 
Anecdote to help you understand: I accidentally dropped my knife at one point and it hit my dress leaving a small but noticeable stain (at my own bachelorette party! I'm not super high maintenance or anything but any girl would frown at such a faux pas). The waiter immediately got me a fresh knife and a napkin along with a glass of seltzer so I could remove the stain...came right out! My hero ! 
Back to business, we ordered some cocktails to start and then a sommelier introduced himself to help us pick out some wine.  We ordered 3 apps for the table: roasted oysters, octopus and lobster.  All three were great but my favorite was the lobster.  I'm usually not big on seafood and this is only the second time or so I've had lobster because I've just never been a fan but the flavors and textures in all these dishes were seriously bomb.  
Moving on to the main dish, I had the steak dianne (pretty sure it was a special that night) and everything from presentation to the last bite was so good. The steak was juicy and flavorful yet not overly heavy and it came with some vegg and potatoes. Classic American dish done so right and they cooked it at the table so we got a little show (according to my fiancee, this was the only all male show we saw that night : )
Dessert was a bananas foster (because these guys literally invented it!) which was prepared table side as well along with complimentary creme brulee and sugar cookies-family style desserts are the best way to end such a meal and even though we were full we persevered...it was all sugary goodness-decadent but not too heavy.
We stayed here ruminating for something like 4 hours...it really felt like we were at a table at a friend's house (a very wealthy friend with a very nice/big house, but still). I would come back for dinner and special occasions. I also hear brunch here is not to be missed.  
I will remember this meal and time with my friends very fondly for a long time to come!

ps not to be tacky but I have to mention that all this for 7 people was around 500 before tip...in my stomping grounds of NYC this would have been double that easily!

Came here for a bachelorette party brunch and it was fantastic! If you are wanting some instagram-worthy photos, this is the place! Located right in the French Quarter and steps from our hotel, it was very easy to find. You can't miss an iconic bright pink building. We made reservations for a party of 8 and were quickly seated in the Audubon room - a Parisian styled room with peach pink walls lined with stunning oyster plates and a beautiful patterned tiled floor. This was our favorite room out of all the rooms! 

Now onto the food. It was our first time here and everything on the menu sounded amazing. Our waitress was so sweet and gave us recommendations on what to order or else we would have ordered the entire menu. Drink wise we all ordered a fruity sparkler (I can't remember the name of it). So we started off with the egg yolk carpaccio with grilled shrimp and sweet potato - very good and the presentation was wonderful. We passed on the turtle soup although I wish I had gotten it now. So instead we got the seafood gumbo (shrimp, andouille, oysters) and it was very tasty. Next I ordered eggs Owen (red wine braised short rib, potatoes, poached eggs, Hollandaise sauce, and Marchand de vin aka red wine reduction sauce). All the flavors were out of this world, the short rib was one of the best I ever had - so tender and juicy with just the right amount of fat! We didn't have any room for dessert but I will definitely be coming back to try the bananas foster one day.

After we got our check we headed out to the courtyard and it felt as if we were walking into a beautiful lush fairytale. The exposed brick along with the lush greenery and the fountain looked stunning. The staff were also very kind by offering to take a bunch of photos for our bachelorette party! This ended up being my favorite restaurant from our whole trip (we visited about 7).

Came here for a fancy bachelorette dinner. Super quaint and quiet place - good for romantic dinners. The service was great and the food was delicious! I would take one star off only because we had made a reservation weeks prior and our table wasn't ready when we got there. And put it back on for their complimentary appetizer and dessert!

Absolutely stellar service, elegant and stately decor and incredible food! Gorgeous huge rose floral display in the center of the room, stunning crystal chandeliers. The dishes were ALL delicious with so much complexity with the preparation process and various components, sous vide, reductions, concentrated sauces, whipped ricotta, mousse...I couldn't begin to remember every explanation of each plate, but that hardly mattered as each bite of every dish was exquisite. The food experience was further enhanced by the traditional and proper cutlery used for each course as well as the knowledge and effusiveness of our waiter, Captain Robert Kerner. He was a delight and played right into our charade of faux Southern names and gaiety as well as bringing out many special surprises for the birthday lady and gentleman.  The cocktails, wine, dessert were also incredible and Captain Robert's vast knowledge of every dish and wine pairing was nothing short of amazing. August was definitely worth every penny and perfect for a special occasion.

We came in to celebrate and birthday and a bachelorette at the same time. Luckily for us, we met one of the owners Dollie. She was having dinner next to our table of 12 girls and she came over to say hi, bought us all a round of champagne. That is southern hospitality that I can say I have never experienced any where else. The level of service was supreme. I've eaten and fancy places in NY and never had my meals served like this before. I loved it because the ambiance was perfect. We stayed past closing (oops) and took lots of pics. They also wrapped up the food in cute little aluminum trinkets that they got creative with.

The food prices were affordable for a 5 star restaruant. I think most people had appetizers to share with an entree which was more than enough. Almost everyone had a little packup aluminum trinket. I had the vegetarian mushroom risotto which was on the salty side, also the word used to describe most entrees. The app that everyone loved was the one with pork belly and fried oyster. I could have ordered that all night if I knew how good it was! They also sent me some balloons to decorate the table which was really cute and a sweet touch. And then brought out a praline sundae for my with Congrats on the plate. I've never really felt so honored at a dinner before so that was one amazing experience. I would definitely come back here for the hospitality and service. Everything came out at the same time, all 12 of our dishes at once.

This restaurant is the perfect spot for events and nice dinners. There are actually two parts of the restaurant (the front and the back) and an outdoor courtyard in the middle. We came here with a party of 7 celebrating a bachelorette and it definitely was the perfect place for our party.

One of the most exemplary points of our dinner was the service. Everything was served at the same time, and the servers were trained extremely well. Even the host was very gracious, and that experience continued throughout until the end. The food and drinks came at a great time, and the server was there when we needed her. The garlic bread was an amazing start to our meal, the bread was warm and crisp and we finished it in minutes. 

We started out with a few appetizers including the five hour egg, mardi foie, and the louisiana oyster fondue. Wow. All of these dishes were very unique, and it's safe to say i've never had anything like these appetizers before.

The Five Hour Egg- a gently coddled egg on roasted mississippi shiitake mushrooms, crisp potato, brown butter vinaigrette and spiced truffle ($13.50)- the egg with the crispy mushrooms and potato on bottom made an amazing combination. Delicious taste and texture. 

Louisiana Oyster Fondue- crispy oysters and brie caulifower fondue with grilled kale, tomatoes, bacon, parmesan, ciabatta ($12.50)- This was very unique as the flavor of the fondue was more on the cajun side. The fondue was very thick, and with the crispy oysters combined, it was my favorite appetizer. 

Mardi Foie- Foie Gras with foie gras cream cheese stuffed beignets, foie gras royal icing, mardi gras sugar and root beer foie gras ice cream float ($18)- So much foie gras! It was a piece of foie gras and a piece of sweet beignet. Did not expect the two to go so well! Especially after eating all of these beignets everywhere... the root beer float also added an interesting flavor, but overall, a great and unique dish. 

We also got the Soups 1-1-1 ($9.50). which included a sampler of the gumbo, turtle, and soup du jour. The gumbo at Louisiana is not what we usually get in California, so it was really interesting to try. The turtle soup was great! I did not expect to like it so much, and it was very unique. I did not remember what the last soup was, but it was a creamy soup of some kind. All three were great though .Would definitely recommend this if you want to try their variety of soups.

For the entrees, all of us got the french white truffle and winter mushroom risotto ($26) with lobster (+$12). The risotto was very creamy and was cooked to perfection, and the lobster really added a nice touch to the risotto. Love how they utilize their truffle here! Too bad this dish was heavy.. we could not finish our food.

For dessert, we got both the souffle and the strawberry shortcake. We thought both were good, but nothing special. Both are made on the spot. 

Overall, a great dinner and fine dining experience at Commander's Palace. I could see why it's on the to go to lists on many blogs!

I came here with a group of friends for a bachelorette party dinner. The restaurant is huge with many rooms and perfect for big groups. The interior had a nice old charm to it, characteristic of New Orleans as a city. There were balloons at tables that were celebrating birthdays, which was a nice touch. We were impressed by how one of the staff members was so attentive in the beginning to change our napkins from white to black when she saw that we were wearing black dresses.
