However, our waiter was not very friendly throughout our meal. Most of our party ordered from Chef Tory's Three Course Offerings. The gumbo and turtle soups were okay but not particularly impressive. For entrees, my friend and I shared the Wagyu beef, which wasn't as marbled as we would have liked, but still tender with delicious mashed potatoes as an accompaniment. We also ordered the crispy soft shell crab, which was crispy as advertised with a nice flavor. We were very excited to try the Creole bread pudding souffle as we had heard rave reviews from a friend - "I dream about that soufflé" were her exact words, so who wouldn't have high expectations?! While it was a good dessert, we didn't feel that it was spectacular, and the whiskey cream sauce was too strong for our taste. I really liked the chewy top of the soufflé though! I'm not a fan of cooked fruit, but actually felt that the roasted apple and caramel cobbler was better. Overall, we had a pleasant dining experience and feel that the food and prices, with the ambience, make this restaurant a good option for nice meals or celebrations.

Let me just start off by saying that I'm a vegetarian, so for me to be giving a seafood place five stars is a big deal. I planned my best friend's bachelorette dinner here a few nights ago and everything about it was perfect. All of my interactions with the staff, even starting with the phone call to confirm the reservation, were really friendly. They gave us a table for ten right in the middle of the room, and they had confetti and a special menu printed up with the bride-to-be's name on the table waiting for us with zero prompting from me. The manager stopped by to say hello and make sure we were all having fun. Our waiter, Troy, charmed everyone's socks off before he could even tell us about the specials. And the food -- literally everyone cleaned their plates. My friends are still talking about the lobster dumplings and scallibut. Even my vegetarian gnocchi was incredible, and even the vegan plate they whipped up for my friend had the carnivores in our party drooling and asking to try her fried leeks. The mashed sweet potatoes with bourbon, vanilla, and banana were probably the most delicious food I tried in New Orleans. The salty malty ice cream pie was killer. Basically this was 100% the perfect spot for a celebration: great food, great atmosphere, great people. And even though it was a great spot for a party, it's definitely not rowdy or noisy. It's the perfect mix of fun and upscale.

We made reservations in advance in honor of our bachelorette.   Upon arrival, our table had flowers and confetti, with a personalized menu congratulating her (along with complementary dessert later). 
The service was terrific and highly attentive.  We enjoyed our meals and fantastic drinks.
 I loved the scallops with mushroom risotto.  Absolutely decadent!
GW Fins was the perfect restaurant to celebrate our big occasion! I highly recommend it

My best girl friends took me here for our fancy dinner night out during my bachelorette last summer, and it was a classy affair of delicious food and drinks, accompanied with fantastic service. 

Must order/try:
* LOBSTER DUMPLINGS
* SCALIBUT

After so many months, I am still gushing about this memorable meal. The lobster dumplings were meaty with the most delicate wonton skin where each bite just melted in my mouth. The scalibut blew our minds away with its perfectly seared scallop crust over a normally mild white fish. It was served with creamy LOBSTER risotto that paired so very well with the fish. The girls and I are from Boston --home of some pretty damn good lobster dishes-- but now I'm starting to think otherwise...

My husband also had his bachelor party in NOLA in the fall, and being good boys, they took our recommendation and had an equally excellent dinner at GW Fins.

+1 star for a personalized keepsake menu and glitter confetti on our table to make us feel extra special :)

results.question: wheelchair accessible restaurant that caters events
results.answer:
Faubourg Bistro is a top choice for event catering, especially for weddings. The review mentions that Matt and his team provided excellent service, helping with setup, teardown, and even suggesting menu options to ensure a smooth event. The food was a hit with all guests, and the attention to detail made the event run seamlessly.

Cane and Table offers a unique event space with a focus on cocktails and delicious food. The event space was praised for its lovely setting and attentive service, with guests raving about the food and drinks. The staff was highly accommodating and helped make the event stress-free, making it a great option for those looking to host a special event.
contexts: I've always loved the food that Faubourg Bistro has done at 700 Club. It's always been inventive, delicious, and fresh. Matt and his team catered my wedding in Slidell for 160 people plus and I cannot say enough good things. From the very beginning Matt made suggestions to help the flow of the evening, presented an incredible menu to choose from, and I credit so much to him and his team as to why the wedding went so smoothly. All of my guests raved about the food. Matt's team also went above and beyond helping us set up and tear down the event. Matt checked out the venue multiple items and his care and attention to detail and planning meant the world. If you are in need of a caterer for an event, there is nowhere else you should seek out. And if you're not, just go by 700 Club and get some of their amazing food!! :D

It was great place to eat with no waiting during Essence Festival 2018.  We had a party of 8 for Saturday brunch. Joey was our waiter and he did a fantastic job taking care of us. He had great suggestion for $20 bottomless vodka bar and menu items. The food was great from the French toast, mac n crawfish, shrimp & grits, chicken & biscuits. I would definitely visit this place again and request Joey.

Excellent food & service!
Had a 4:30 reservation on a Saturday before a concert. Was seated immediately. Server came to take our drink orders & answered any questions.
Had one in our party that has a Gluten allergy, she had the grilled fish (redfish) & she loved it. I had the skirt steak & it was excellent. 
Service was outstanding. I'll be back.

Had a wonderful experience ordering catering for a party of 15 flawlessly delivered to our hotel room for a reasonable fee. From the moment I called, to the day of, there was nothing but kindness and professionalism.  We had a variety of cheese and meat pies, salad, garlic bread, and cookies ordered: everything was soooo good. They make their namesake proud!

I WILL be back! Let me just start off by saying add yourself to the waiting list via Yelp because you WILL wait.  You can check the status of your wait time via the link that is texted to you. I went during Essence festival weekend and the wait was beyond long.  But the rum punch and bottled water being sold outside the restaurant and the DJ definitely made the wait a bit easier. All of the staff that we encountered was very cool under pressure and service was excellent.  Once we finally got seated and ordered, the food came out quickly.

Char-broiled oysters were delightful.  A friend and I shared a combo meal that came with gumbo, salad and a platter of seafood delectables and desert.  Needless to say there was no room for desert so we took it to go.

I worked with Justin to arrange a large cocktail style reception for over 30 guest. He was extremely accommodating, the setting was lovely, the drinks and food were delicious, and the service (especially Cindy) was incredibly attentive and kind. Thank you for putting up with our party! Couldn't have been better. 

PS: the ribs are deep fried, and you want them in your life.

Cane and Table has always been one of my husband and I's favorite cocktail bars. So when it was time to plan our wedding, it was one of the first places we called. This past weekend, we had our wedding reception upstairs in their new event space. Our guests were raving about the service, food and specialty cocktails. The staff was so friendly and helpful in planning, setting up and executing the event. We really couldn't have been happier with how everything turned out. I'd highly recommend to anyone looking to throw an impressive event with great food and drinks. Thanks so much to Leila and the rest of the Cane and Table team for making our day so fun and stress free.

results.question: dressy dinner spot with live music
results.answer:
Arnaud's French 75 Bar is a fantastic choice for a dressy dinner spot with live music. The place exudes old-world charm with its classic tile floors, beveled mirrors, and monkey lamps, making it feel like stepping into another era. The staff is professional and friendly, and the cocktails are top-notch, though the dress code could be more formal. 

Foundation Room offers a unique and intimate experience with its Art Deco decor, live jazz trio, and a cozy lounge area. The atmosphere is mysterious and exclusive, perfect for a romantic evening. The food and service are excellent, though the venue is more of a private club, which might be a factor to consider. 

Chophouse New Orleans provides a more traditional steakhouse experience with a great ambiance and excellent food. The piano player sets a relaxing tone, and the food quality is outstanding, though it's on the pricier side.
contexts: Elegant but not stuffy is the best description of this gem, connected to the excellent Arnaud's Restaurant. Staff is very professional , impeccably dressed and friendly making excellent cocktails, and serving bar foods off the restaurant menu, they will answer any question regarding cocktails and food and with make recommendations according to your likes. Sunday evening is my usual time because not as crowded but anytime is good. It's almost like stepping into another time because with the old tile floor, beautiful beveled mirrors and windows, funny monkey lamps and the old French "café" music it's as if you back in 1920 Paris in fact you almost expect to see Hemingway in the corner eating raw oysters and drinking Pastis. The only thing that makes the elegance not "quite deliver" is the dress of the customers.

Now I can completely relate to Owen Wilson's character in Midnight in Paris. Walking into an age of prohibition and art decor decor with snappy dressed waiters now offering me the cigar menu to pair with my drink. 
Whether you are the drinking buff or perhaps just coming for the Orleans nightcap you must surely pay your passage way into here. 
I can see a modern Scott and Zelda Fitzgerald hunkering over in the corner next to the monkey lampshades drinking it up with the cigar bar in full use. 
The place is ideal for bringing the tres-romantic out oneself and you'll see plenty of couples, but don't be dismayed as there are plenty of dapper don draper's sitting in random corners of the room having their brandy and cigars only to add to the atmosphere. 
Now go drown out your last night in some Oysters Rockefeller.

Classy. Sophisticated. Timeless. Intimate. If you want to feel like you just walked onto the set of the movie Midnight in Paris, go to Arnaud's French 75.

Everything about this bar was perfect. I'm really into cocktails and try to see lots of bars on my vacations, and this was quite possibly the nicest bar I have ever had the pleasure of visiting. My boyfriend and I came in shortly past 11pm on a weekday and there were only a couple of other people inside. We were seated at a small table and while we tried to decide which drinks to get, I admired the classic tile floors and eccentric monkey lamps. A young man in a tuxedo served us water. We settled on a French 75 and a Sazerac and they did not disappoint- stiff and made with premium spirits. 

All the proportions and textures of the interior were so pleasing- from the impressive, intricately carved wood counter, to the the height of the ceiling and the mirror on the back wall. It just felt really good to sit there and take it all in. Adding to the ambiance was the heady aroma of cigar smoke. Usually I really don't like the smell of cigars, but there, it just fit.

The French 75 Bar is a special, special place!

Home run! Best meal, ambiance and experience maybe ever for me, my wife, plus one. Cocktails at the bar probably enhanced our experience. The piano player set a relaxing tone. Reserved table was on time. Food quality was the highlight. Fairly pricey.

Stopped here for a late afternoon drink. Great space reminiscent of an old school New York City steakhouse, but with much more charm. Great selection of scotches and a really good pianist/vocalist at just the right level to still have a conversation. Good drinks reasonably priced and good service.
Excellent food and service,  I would recommend it. Piano player was off the charts. Handsome waiter what more can I say

The ambiance of this place is like nothing I've seen before. They had a cozy lounge with chesterfield sofas, a full bar serving prohibition-era cocktails, and a live jazz trio. You enter the Foundation Room through a highly stylized Art Deco corridor and elevator, proceeded by a stained glass corridor. The walls are upholstered with Indian blankets embroidered with tiny mirrors and the carpeting is made of Persian rugs tacked together. The dining area features a domed ceiling and a wall of temperature controlled wine. The host staff made the experience feel very mysterious and exclusive, inviting us to the lounge and serving us champagne while we waited for our table. The waitress and kitchen accommodated my dietary restrictions (pescatarian, dairy-free). The tuna tartare appetizer special was tasty, and the salad was fine. The lettuces were very nice but I held the cheese so it wasn't anything too special. My fish and veggie entree was tasty and was made special for me to meet my dietary needs. The cocktails were also tasty - I did the Cider cocktail and a French 75. After dinner we hung out in the uncrowded lounge and enjoyed some more tasty libations while listening to the jazz trio. Quintessential New Orleans cocktail bar experience.

Normally, I would find myself on the other side of the velvet ropes at places like this, but a friend "put me on the list" to see her girlfriend's jazzy lounge band, Soulkestra, play a few sets.  So I have visited the Foundation Room three times, and surprisingly I was able to set aside the anti-elitist hang-ups that accompany my low birth and actually enjoy myself there.  

In fact, it was not until my third visit that I learned during a conversation with one of the paying members that this was actually a private venue.  I just assumed it was HOB's attempt at an "intimate jazz venue."  While I would not pay money to purposefully seclude myself in a private bar, as my life is thankfully devoid of paparrazi and hangers-on, I can certainly see the appeal.  The barmen are pros, the drinks are properly stiff, and there is a stairway leading to a private-access balcony overlooking the HOB main stage.  

The lounge's decor is wall-to-wall neo-opium den chic, complete with a replica "Reclining Buddha" statue on the hearth of the artificial fire.  There are plush couches and lounge chairs and even a bottle-service area that I assume is an anti-malarial quarantine because its semicircular booth is partitioned with mosquito netting.  Most impressive to me, however, was the carpeting, which a manager explained to me was actually not carpeting per se but dozens and dozens of Persian rugs stitched together to cover the entire lounge.

There is typically a pretty constant stream of mini-skirted "woo girls" and the untucked-oxford shirted men that tend to surround them.  If that's your thing, well go get em, tiger, but you'll have to plunk down the coin.  As for me, I'll just enjoy a couple gin drinks and some good music in this cool, relaxing lounge.  3.5 rounded up.

Outstanding atmosphere.  Small quiet dining room right outside a very classy but eclectically decorated live music bar to enjoy before dinner.  

Great food and service.
